title: JAX_DPC: scRNA sequencing using 10X Chromium Fixed RNA Profiling
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aoa2f","text":"This protocol outlines the use of the Chromium Fixed RNA Profiling Reagent Kits (10x Genomics) for single-cell gene expression analysis of multiplexed samples derived from cortical brain organoids, across a three-day consecutive time course.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Chromium Fixed RNA Profiling for Multiplexed Samples
Dissociate differentiated iPSCs into single cells
The reagents and process workflow are described below: https://cdn.10xgenomics.com/image/upload/v1694503892/support-documents/CG000527_ChromiumFixedRNAProfiling_MultiplexedSamples_UserGuide_Rev_E.pdf
Centrifuge sample at 350 rcf for 5 min at 4°C.
Add 1 ml Fixation Buffer to the sample pellet and pipette mix 5x.
Remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.
Incubate for 1 h at room temperature (20°C) or for 16-24 h at 4°C for long term storage.
Centrifuge at 850 rcf for 5 min at room temperature.
Remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.
Add 1 ml chilled Quenching Buffer to the sample pellet and pipette mix 5x and keep on ice.
Determine cell concentration of the fixed sample using AO/PI (acridine orange/propidium iodide) Cell Viability Kit for Luna-FL automated cell counter.
Thaw Enhancer (10x Genomics PN-2000482) for 10 min at 65°C. Vortex and centrifuge briefly. Keep warm and verify no precipitate before use.
Add 1 volume pre-warmed Enhancer to fixed sample in Quenching Buffer. Pipette mix.
Store sample at 4°C for up to 1 week. For long term storage (up to 6 months) also add 50% glycerol for a final concentration of 10% with Quenching buffer and store at -80°C.
creator: Juliana Alcoforado Diniz
